relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Peter writes to those sharing equally precious trust.
Grace and peace multiply through knowing Divine and Jesus.
Divine power grants all things for life and reverence.
Promises make participation in divine nature possible.
Trust supplies excellence.
Knowledge matures into self-control and endurance.
Reverence opens into sibling-love and love.
Increasing virtues make knowledge fruitful.
Forgetting cleansing narrows sight.
Calling and choosing are made firm through practice.
The reign entrance is richly supplied.
Reminder keeps truth present.
Peter awakens them while still in the tent.
The tent will soon be put off.
Remembrance is prepared for after departure.
The apostolic witness did not follow crafted myths.
The beloved Son receives honor and glory.
The heavenly voice was heard on the holy mountain.
The prophetic word shines like a lamp until morning star rises in hearts.
Prophecy is not private release.
Humans spoke from Divine, carried by holy Spirit.

contemplative_note: 2 Peter 1 is participatory ascent: divine promises, virtue maturing into love, and prophetic light shining until the morning star rises within.
